svg.title: Domain credibility tiers with weights and domain diversity penalty rules
svg.desc: Eight credibility tiers from government sources at 1.0 down to social media at 0.05, plus domain diversity multipliers for repeated domains.
svg.text: CREDIBILITY WEIGHT TIERS — CW value per domain
TEMPORAL DECAY WEIGHT — TW by article age
Exception: HISTORY and SCIENCE domain claims → TW = 1.00 regardless of age. Historical facts do not decay.
DOMAIN DIVERSITY MULTIPLIER — applied during evidence selection

g.text: Hard cap: max 3 pieces of evidence from any single root domain. 4th+ → filtered out entirely.

svg.text: This is where MMR (Maximal Marginal Relevance) is correctly applied — during evidence selection,
not during query generation. MMR balances relevance vs diversity across your evidence pool.
All weights multiply together: EW(e) = RS × CW × TW · then domain diversity multiplier applied first during selection.
